sanskrit: अभ्यासेऽप्यसमर्थोऽसि मत्कर्मपरमो भव। मदर्थमपि कर्माणि कुर्वन् सिद्धिमवाप्स्यसि॥
transliteration: abhyāse ’py asamartho ’si mat-karma-paramo bhava | mad-artham api karmāṇi kurvan siddhim avāpsyasi ||
literal_meaning: If you are unable even to practice, then make My work your highest goal; performing actions for My sake, you will attain perfection.
contextual_meaning: When disciplined practice feels impossible, shift from self-referencing effort to service-oriented action; redirecting motivation toward a purpose larger than the ego dissolves performance anxiety and restores agency.
psychological_state: Learned-helplessness transitioning to vicarious self-efficacy through meaningful contribution.
trigger_scenario: A burned-out caregiver who can’t sustain meditation or yoga still finds recovery by cooking meals at a shelter, discovering calm through usefulness.
anchor_text: Work for something bigger than yourself and perfection follows.
